content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a base scaffold to unify fragmented multiple-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical self-refinement system where different inconsistent solving perspectives act as structured self-feedback sources for iterative improvement across iterations. This structure lets the system retains Self-Refine's key advantage of no extra supervised training or reinforcement learning, while leveraging perspective-driven reflection resolves the limitation of single-perspective blind spots that plague vanilla Self-Refine.
